name: Mirabel Castle Loop
name_original: Subida al Castillo de Mirabel — circular desde Maribel
description: This moderate 4.1-mile jogging route from Maribel ascends to Castillo de Mirabel, offering great views and varied terrain.
region_intro: Jogging around Mirabel offers access to diverse parks and extensive trail networks. The region features urban forest parks like Parc du Domaine Vert, providing varied terrain for running. Additionally, areas such as Bois de Belle-Rivière Regional Park offer picturesque forest trails. Mirabel also connects to the P'tit Train du Nord, a linear park with scenic views of agricultural landscapes. Best jogging routes around Mirabel The most popular jogging route is Garganta del Fraile Loop, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This route presents a moderate challenge with varied elevation. Another top favourite among local runners is Umbría del Barbechoso – running circular from Mirabel, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through natural landscapes. Local runners also love the Mirabel Castle Loop, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through historical surroundings, often completed in about 50 minutes. Jogging around Mirabel is defined by urban forest parks, extensive trail networks, and scenic agricultural landscapes. The region offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Mirabel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Mirabel?
A: Mirabel offers a selection of 11 dedicated running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the region's diverse parks and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Mirabel?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, Mirabel's extensive trail networks, particularly within parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parc du Domaine Vert&lt;/strong&gt;, often include paved or less challenging sections suitable for beginners. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196736034/garganta-del-fraile-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Fraile Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.9 km long.

Q: Can I run on the P'tit Train du Nord near Mirabel?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;P'tit Train du Nord&lt;/strong&gt; is a prominent linear park that passes through the Mirabel area and is very popular for jogging and other activities. It offers scenic views of agricultural landscapes and is part of Quebec's extensive Route Verte cycling network, providing a generally flat and accessible surface for runners.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging paths in Mirabel?
A: Absolutely. Parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parc du Domaine Vert&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Bois de Belle-Rivière Regional Park&lt;/strong&gt; are known for their picturesque environments and varied trails, many of which are suitable for families. These parks often feature amenities and natural settings that make for an enjoyable outing with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on running trails in Mirabel?
A: Many outdoor areas in Mirabel, including sections of the larger parks and regional trails, are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to check specific park regulations or trail signage for leash requirements and any restricted areas before heading out with your canine companion.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Mirabel?
A: Yes, Mirabel offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298253991/mirabel-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.5 km circular trail that takes you through historical surroundings. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991211828/umbria-del-barbechoso-running-circular-from-mirabel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría del Barbechoso – running circular from Mirabel&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.1 km path with significant elevation gain.

Q: What can I see along the jogging routes in Mirabel?
A: Running routes in Mirabel offer a mix of natural beauty and historical interest. You can experience urban forest parks, tranquil forested areas, and scenic agricultural landscapes. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298253991/mirabel-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, take you past historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; and natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Mirabel?
A: Mirabel is suitable for outdoor activities year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for running. Summer provides warm weather for those who enjoy it, while winter offers opportunities for cold-weather jogging, especially on well-maintained or plowed trails.

Q: Are there options for winter jogging in Mirabel?
A: Yes, Mirabel's parks and trail networks can be enjoyed in winter. While some trails might be covered in snow, popular linear parks like the &lt;strong&gt;P'tit Train du Nord&lt;/strong&gt; are often maintained or packed down, making them suitable for winter running. Always check local conditions and dress appropriately for the weather.

Q: Are there jogging routes with easy parking in Mirabel?
A: Many of Mirabel's larger parks, such as &lt;strong&gt;Parc du Domaine Vert&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Bois de Belle-Rivière Regional Park&lt;/strong&gt;, are designed with visitor access in mind and typically offer ample parking facilities near trailheads. The &lt;strong&gt;P'tit Train du Nord&lt;/strong&gt; also has various access points with parking along its length.

Q: How long are the running routes in Mirabel?
A: The running routes in Mirabel vary in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298253991/mirabel-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 6.5 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295171231/canal-del-rio-alagon-by-the-ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-argamasa-circular-from-riolobos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal del Río Alagón by the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de La Argamasa&lt;/a&gt; route is approximately 14 km. You can find routes ranging from moderate distances to longer, more challenging runs.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Mirabel's diverse landscapes provide opportunities for scenic views. While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every running route, trails through forested areas and along the &lt;strong&gt;P'tit Train du Nord&lt;/strong&gt; offer picturesque vistas of the natural environment and agricultural fields. For broader scenic attractions, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5457032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Gitano (Vulture Cliff)&lt;/a&gt;, which are nearby.
tips: The origin of the castle of Mirabel or Peña del Acero dates back to the 15th century. Now it is for the most part in ruins. The views of the surroundings from this point are spectacular.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ascent to Castillo de Mirabel jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While suitable for jogging, it requires good fitness and sure-footedness, especially on ascents and potentially uneven surfaces. It's not a casual run, so be prepared for a sustained physical challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter mostly accessible paths, including sections of 'Path', 'Hiking Path', 'Street', and 'Road'. Surfaces range from 'Natural' and 'Unpaved' to 'Paved' and 'Asphalt'. Joggers should expect smooth stretches alongside more rugged, natural sections requiring careful footing.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque natural landscapes within the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere, including Mediterranean forests and open countryside. A notable natural feature is the ancient 'Padre Santo' cork oak. The highlight of the route is reaching the Mirabel Castle, which offers panoramic vistas of the surrounding region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Monfragüe National Park Biosphere is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions. Check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to Castillo de Mirabel trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on varied terrain. Always check specific park regulations regarding pets before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: Typically, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the trails leading to Castillo de Mirabel within the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or seasonal restrictions before your trip.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ascent to Castillo de Mirabel trail?
faqs.answer: For this circular route starting from Maribel, you can usually find parking within the village itself. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots near the trailhead. Specific parking details might vary, so plan to arrive early, especially on busy days.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty and varied terrain, this route might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to sustained physical activity. Older children with good fitness and sure-footedness could enjoy it, but it's not considered an 'easy' family walk. Shorter, more direct ascents to the castle might be more suitable for families seeking an easier option.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route passes through sections of the official trail 'Umbría del Barbechoso', which is part of the broader network within the Monfragüe National Park Biosphere. This offers opportunities to explore other paths if you wish to extend your adventure.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate jogging route, bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is advisable, especially during warmer months. A small first-aid kit and a charged phone are also recommended for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Maribel?
faqs.answer: Maribel is a small village, and while specific cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead might be limited, you can usually find local establishments within the village itself where you can grab refreshments or a meal before or after your run. It's best to check local listings for current operating hours.
